result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates a JSON schema from a .NET class.

Usage:
  SharpSchema.Tool [options]

Options:
  -a, --assembly <assembly> (REQUIRED)                         The assembly containing the root class to generate a schema for.
  -c, --class <class> (REQUIRED)                               The root class to generate a schema for.
  -r, --reference <reference>                                  An assembly to reference.
  -d, --reference-directory <reference-directory>              A directory containing assemblies to reference.
  -t, --directory-recursion-depth <directory-recursion-depth>  The maximum depth to recurse when searching for assemblies in a reference directory. [default: 1]
  --enum-as-underlying-type                                    Use the underlying type of an enum in the schema instead of strings. [default: False]
  --parse-doc-comments                                         Parse XML documentation comments. [default: False]
  --max-depth <max-depth>                                      The maximum depth to traverse when converting types. [default: 50]
  -o, --output <output>                                        The directory to write the generated schemas to. If no path is provided, outputs to the current directory. When this option is not provided, outputs to the console.
  --pretty-printed                                             Pretty print the output JSON. [default: True]
  --strict-json-escaping                                       Use strict JSON escaping. [default: False]
  --overwrite                                                  Overwrite the output file if it exists. [default: False]
  -v, --verbosity <Diagnostic|Normal|Quiet>                    Set the verbosity level. Without a value, sets the level to Diagnostic. [default: Normal]
  -q, --quiet                                                  Suppress all output except for errors. Overrides the --verbosity option. [default: False]
  --version                                                    Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                               Show help and usage information

  --pretty-printed                                             Pretty print the output JSON. [default: True]

  --overwrite                                                  Overwrite the output file if it exists. [default: False]
  -v, --verbosity <Diagnostic|Normal|Quiet>                    Set the verbosity level. Without a value, sets the level to Diagnostic. [default: Normal]
  -q, --quiet                                                  Suppress all output except for errors. Overrides the --verbosity option. [default: False]
